someone fact check me on this please this is very important stuff <:Murphy_satisfy:1468258389850193960>

the city is called shencheng, a city wth "winding alleys" where magnolia grows in "stifling heat"

They also use shanghai slang like muma and nannan

The master's name is romanized Ho Fong which is not a northern chinese pronunciation

Shencheng has "modern neon" which was extremely rare for china in this time period. Shanghai and Hong Kong are the cities that were famous for having neon lighting in the 1920s

> So there, I am, in Sri Lanka, formerly Ceylon, at about 3 o'clock in the morning, looking for one thousand small cakes, or Liz wouldn't go on stage that night. So, Dexter pops his head 'round the door, and mentions there's a little sweets shop on the edge of town. So - we go. And - it's closed. So there's me, and Jenkins, and Uvhash, breaking into that little sweets shop, eh. Well, instead of a guard dog, they've got this bloody great big bengal tiger. I managed to take out the tiger with a mace, but the shopkeeper and his son... that's a different story altogether. I had to beat them to death with their own shoes. Nasty business, really, but sure enough I got the small cakes, and Liz went on stage and did a great show.
-Keeper probably
